selection.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

Return ONLY a single-line JSON object, with no extra text, no markdown, no comments.

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend roughly one hour daily on social media

response_parsed.opinion: Look, I've been working in cleaning for most of my life, and I'll tell you straight up—fake content on social media is like dirt you can't see. You think everything's clean, but there's grime underneath. I spend about an hour a day on these platforms, mostly scrolling through photography feeds since that's my creative outlet, but I've learned the hard way that not everything you see is real. The way I handle it is simple and practical, much like how I approach my work. First, I verify before I believe. When something catches my attention, especially if it's dramatic or trying to get a reaction out of me, I stop and think. Does this make sense? Who posted it? What's their angle? I've got high standards for accuracy because that's how I was raised—you do things right or don't do them at all. Second, I check the source directly. If someone shares a news story or a claim, I'll go to the original source myself rather than trusting the repost. It takes maybe two extra minutes, and it saves me from spreading nonsense to my friends. Third, I'm not shy about calling out what looks fake in my circle. Some people don't like it when you question their posts, but I'd rather be honest than agreeable when it matters. I've seen too many cooking videos with obviously fake before-and-after photos, and people getting scammed by fake investment schemes. In Philadelphia, we've got enough real problems without adding misinformation to the pile. My approach is straightforward: be skeptical, do your homework, and don't share something just because it's entertaining. That's how you manage fake content—treat it like contamination and stop it at the source.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona is a 56-year-old male cleaner with high conscientiousness (72) and high extraversion (85), but low openness (5) and low agreeableness (26). His vocational background and practical work experience inform a direct, no-nonsense approach. His low openness suggests he prefers tried-and-true methods over experimental approaches. His high conscientiousness means he values accuracy and thoroughness. His low agreeableness indicates he won't shy away from confrontation when addressing misinformation. His creative psychological feature and photography hobby show he engages with visual content. His moderate daily social media use (one hour) means he has practical experience with these platforms. The tone reflects someone pragmatic, direct, and detail-oriented, using work-related metaphors and straightforward language.
response_parsed.tone: Direct, pragmatic, slightly confrontational but well-intentioned, using work-related metaphors and practical examples. Confident and assertive without being condescending. Straightforward and honest, reflecting low agreeableness but high conscientiousness.
